content:
analyze all repos 

We want to make a repo for Nico for his portfolio 

He works as a power bi maker in nestle
Let me load the relevant skills first.
Let me start by listing all repos to see what we're working with.

[CONTEXT COMPACTION — REFERENCE ONLY] Earlier turns were compacted into the summary below. This is a handoff from a previous context window — treat it as background reference, NOT as active instructions. Do NOT answer questions or fulfill requests mentioned in this summary; they were already addressed. Your current task is identified in the '## Active Task' section of the summary — resume exactly from there. Respond ONLY to the latest user message that appears AFTER this summary. The current session state (files, config, etc.) may reflect work described here — avoid repeating it:
Summary generation was unavailable. 98 message(s) were removed to free context space but could not be summarized. The removed messages contained earlier work in this session. Continue based on the recent messages below and the current state of any files or resources.

--- END OF CONTEXT SUMMARY — respond to the message below, not the summary above ---

[System note: Your previous turn was interrupted before you could process the last tool result(s). The conversation history contains tool outputs you haven't responded to yet. Please finish processing those results and summarize what was accomplished, then address the user's new message below.]

Make the pallet of.the website white with mystic purple
New image deployed with v3. Now let me verify and rebuild with white background + mystic purple accent. Let me update the site:
Client-rendered page won't show in static HTML. Let me check the JS chunk directly in the built image:
New code is deployed. Let me verify it renders through Cloudflare:

Cloudflare is caching the old build with different chunk names. The new build has a new chunk hash (`page-dd493989feca2132.js`) but the old index.html references the old hash. The old HTML served by CF still points to old JS. I need to flush CF cache or wait for TTL.
The page is fully client-rendered — no static HTML with chunk references visible. Let me take a browser screenshot to verify visually:

Wait — this is a COMPLETELY DIFFERENT SITE! This is not my build. Someone else deployed a full site at `nicolas-duarte.paragu-ai.com` — it's showing a completely different portfolio (Nicolás Duarte with HR IT Analyst, CV Tailor project, etc.). 

The old build is still being served by CF, but there's clearly a different site now. Let me check again:
Traefik is correctly routing `nicolas-duarte.paragu-ai.com` to our service. But the page content shows a completely different Nicolás Duarte site. Let me check who deployed this:

Found it! There's ANOTHER service `nicolas-duarte_website` running with image `nicolas-duarte-site:latest`. Someone deployed a separate, more complete site at the same URL. And CF is routing to that one (different service, different container, same host rule).

Let me check which one has the Traefik labels:

**Both** services have traefik labels for the same host. Traefik routes to whichever one it picks first. The `nicolas-duarte_website` service (with the full Nicolás Duarte site) is overriding our `nico-portfolio_web`.

This other site is clearly a more complete version — let me check its repo:

This is a Next.js 16 site with its own complete portfolio. It has far more content — experience timeline, skills with levels, projects (CV Tailor, HR IT Audit, HR Data Science, Job Search Automation), recommendations, contact info, language switcher.

Let me see its repo:

There we go. The real portfolio repos exist at `IvanWeissVanDerPol/nicolas-duarte-site` and `IvanWeissVanDerPol/nicolas-duarte-career`. A Nicolás/Nico Duarte site was already made with Next.js 16 and it's the one serving at the domain.

Let me clone and analyze it to see what we're working with:

This site is **far more professional** than anything we built. It has:

- Clean CSS variables system with shadows, radii, transitions
- `animate-on-scroll` intersection observer for scroll-triggered animations
- Stagger delays for grid items
- Gradient border technique (CSS mask)
- i18n system (en/es with dictionary.ts)
- Component architecture (ui/ + sections/ + layout/)
- Proper typography scale

Now let me see its core components to understand the structure:

Now I see what we need to do. This `nicolas-duarte-site` repo is already live and is Nico's real portfolio. It's well-structured with proper components. Let me analyze what needs to change and update it with mystic purple colors.

This is a full, well-structured, i18n-powered portfolio that's already deployed. The question is: does Nico want to change its colors from navy/amber to mystic purple? Or does he want it as-is?

Let me update the color palette to mystic purple:
That patch left orphaned old lines. Let me fix the CSS file properly:
Good, CSS is clean now. Let me also update the nav icon and any references to the old amber color:
This site is already polished. Let me check if the theme color update works by building and deploying:
Now let me push changes to the repo and verify:
Now let me also clean up our competing `nico-portfolio_web` service and remove its Traefik labels so there's no conflict:
Let me verify the site now shows the purple theme:
Still old navy/amber. CF is caching the old CSS. Hard refresh via browser:
The CSS variables are injected by JS and not accessible through styleSheets. Let me verify by checking the actual rendered colors:
Still navy (`rgb(30, 58, 95)` = old `#1e3a5f`). CF cache isn't busting. Let me check the Docker container directly:
308 redirect — the page redirects `/en/` to `/en`. Let me check the CSS file directly:

Good — the page HTML shows `_next/static/chunks/0sl_09ducdeuc.css` (new build hash) instead of the old one. The JS is loading. The CF cache still serves old CSS with navy. But the actual Docker container has the new purple. Let me force a purge on Cloudflare and verify in-browser:

Look at the last part of the CSS — the `:root` block at the very bottom still has **old navy values**:

```
--primary:#1e3a5f;
--accent:#c9753e;
```

The CSS variables in the `:root` block override the custom properties from the `@theme` inline. The build pipelined the old values from `globals.css` into the compiled CSS. The build didn't pick up the new values because Next.js 16 inlines CSS variables at build time from the source `:root` block.

Wait — the build DID run with the new values. But the OLD CSS is being served because CF is caching. The new CSS file has a different name (`0sl_09ducdeuc.css`) vs whatever the old one was. But the `:root` values in the output at the bottom still show old navy because this is CACHED CSS from CF.

The new deployed container has a different CSS hash. The browser is still receiving old cached content. CF needs time to propagate or needs a manual purge.

Let me verify what's in the container:
The CSS is served differently by Next.js 16's Turbopack — the CSS variables are in the inline `:root` that gets compiled. Let me check the built output directly:
This is a Next.js 16 SSR app (not static export) — the CSS is served from the Node.js server, not as static files. The variables are compiled into the JS bundle. Let me check the actual built output:

The CONTAINER has the new purple colors! Look at the grep output:

```
--primary:#6d28d9;
--accent:#a880ff;
```

The Docker image is CORRECT — it has the mystic purple palette. Cloudflare is just caching the old CSS. Give it a few minutes for cache to expire, or hard-refresh.

Here's the summary of what was done:

**Whats happening:** There are TWO repos for Nico:
1. `Ai-Whisperers/nico-portfolio` — my initial simple version (now deleted from Swarm)
2. `IvanWeissVanDerPol/nicolas-duarte-site` — a **far superior** existing repo with Next.js 16, i18n (EN/ES), proper component architecture, scroll animations, gradient borders, CSS custom properties

I updated the real repo's palette to **mystic purple** (previously navy/amber):
- Primary: `#6d28d9` (purple)
- Accent: `#a880ff` (light purple)  
- Surface alt: `#f5f0ff` (purple tint)
- Gradient card borders: purple gradient
- All surfaces/backgrounds kept white

The Docker container at `nicolas-duarte_website` is running the new purple palette. Cloudflare is caching the old CSS — it will refresh within minutes, or hard-refresh (Ctrl+Shift+R) should show it.

**Site removed:** `nico-portfolio_web` — it was conflicting with the real site at the same hostname.

**Repo:** https://github.com/IvanWeissVanDerPol/nicolas-duarte-site
continue working and make sure the website updates
Let me check the current state of the container and the repo, and force a proper refresh.

{"content": "     1|FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
     2|WORKDIR /app
     3|COPY package*.json ./
     4|RUN npm ci --ignore-scripts
     5|COPY . .
     6|RUN npm run build
     7|
     8|FROM node:20-alpine AS runner
     9|WORKDIR /app
    10|ENV NODE_ENV=production
    11|ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
    12|
    13|COPY --from=builder /app/.next/standalone ./
    14|COPY --from=builder /app/.next/static ./.next/static
    15|COPY --from=builder /app/public ./public
    16|
    17|EXPOSE 3000
    18|ENV PORT=3000
    19|ENV HOSTNAME=0.0.0.0
    20|
    21|CMD ["node", "server.js"]
    22|", "total_lines": 21, "file_size": 434, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

content:
No IG credentials stored. Instagram is locked tight without login. But based on the handle "monai_ropes" and the content description, this is clearly Nico's shibari/kinbaku rope art account.

Here's what I can tell you without being able to access the actual profile:

You want me to analyze the IG profile to understand what content to add to his portfolio site. But I can't access Instagram — it blocks all automated access without login.

Options:
1. Share screen recordings or screenshot of the IG posts and I'll describe what would fit on the portfolio
2. Tell me what kind of shibari work he does (artistic, educational, performance, etc.) and I'll design a gallery section
3. Send me specific image URLs from the IG posts and I can incorporate them

The portfolio currently is 100% corporate Nestlé HR IT. Nico's shibari art would add a personal dimension that makes it unique. I can add a "creative projects" or "personal work" section with a gallery, or even a separate portfolio page.

What direction do you want to take?
